title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. Capabilities are parts of the rights generally granted on a Linux system to the root user. In many cases applications running in containers do not require any capabilities to operate, so from the perspective of the principal of least privilege use of capabilities should be minimized.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that at least one policy requires that capabilities are dropped by all containers.
input_row.remediation: Review the use of capabilities in applications running on your cluster. Where a namespace contains applications which do not require any Linux capabilities to operate consider adding a policy which forbids the admission of containers which do not drop all capabilities.
input_row.impact: Pods with containers require capabilities to operate will not be permitted.
input_row.default_value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of containers with additional capabilities

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_capabilities_dropped

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will check the following:

1. **Kubernetes API Calls:**
   - Use the Kubernetes API to list all `PodSecurityPolicies` (if applicable) or `PodSecurityAdmission` configurations.
   - Use `kubectl` to retrieve the current `Pod` specifications across all namespaces.

2. **Kubernetes CLI Commands:**
   - `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json` to retrieve all pods and their specifications.
   - `kubectl get psp` to list all Pod Security Policies (if enabled).

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Pods across all namespaces.
   - Pod Security Policies (if applicable).

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Check the `securityContext` of each Pod to ensure that the `capabilities` field is set to drop all capabilities (i.e., `capabilities: drop: ["ALL"]`).
   - Verify that at least one Pod Security Policy or Pod Security Admission configuration enforces the dropping of capabilities.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** If all Pods have their `securityContext.capabilities.drop` set to `["ALL"]` and at least one Pod Security Policy or Pod Security Admission configuration is present that enforces this requirement.
   - **Fail:** If any Pod does not drop all capabilities or if there is no enforcing policy in place.

6. **Example:**
   - Use `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.securityContext.capabilities}'` to verify that all Pods have capabilities dropped.
